06

Executive Summary

Inspector assessment and recommendations

“
A comprehensive inspection of the property was conducted to assess its overall condition, functionality, and maintenance status. Based on the observations made during the inspection, the property is considered to be in Good Condition. The main structural elements appeared sound, with no significant defects or safety concerns observed at the time of inspection. Internal and external finishes were generally well maintained and showed only minor signs of normal wear and tear consistent with the property's age and use. Building services, including electrical, plumbing, and mechanical systems, were found to be operational, subject to the limitations of a visual inspection. Routine maintenance and minor repairs may be required from time to time to preserve the property's condition; however, no major remedial works are currently considered necessary. Overall, the property presents as a well-maintained asset that is fit for its intended use and demonstrates a satisfactory standard of upkeep.

SOP Confirmation & Declaration
By issuing this report, the inspector confirms that this inspection has been conducted in full accordance with the Luxhav Privé Inspection Protocol and Standard Operating Procedures. No report may be submitted without photographic evidence for each identified issue. All reports are filed in the client’s digital record and form part of the property’s permanent maintenance history. This report is confidential and prepared exclusively for Mr Halvorsen.
